commands.review_summary:
The PR passes the effective Codex run timeout into the terminal-idle watchdog resolver and adds focused tests for default, longer-budget, shorter-budget, override, and non-finite cases.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main shows the terminal-idle resolver falls back to the 30-minute constant without considering params.timeoutMs, so a longer run-specific timeout can still be superseded by that internal watchdog; I did not live-wait a 30-minute scheduled job.

target.title: fix(codex): derive terminal-idle watchdog from effective run timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85242. Derives the Codex app-server terminal-idle watchdog from the effective run timeout instead of a hardcoded 30-minute default.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: The Codex app-server terminal-idle watchdog used a hardcoded 30-minute default (CODEX_TURN_TERMINAL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS) not derived from the effective run timeout, so a scheduled turn configured with a longer timeoutSeconds was aborted early at 30 minutes. This derives the watchdog from params.timeoutMs.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Invoked resolveCodexTurnTerminalIdleTimeoutMs directly via tsx against the real code through the testing export.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the resolver against the real runtime via tsx.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the tsx invocation against the real runtime:

```
$ npx tsx proof.ts
resolve(undefined) -> 1800000 ms   (no run budget; keeps the 30 minute default; backward compatible)
resolve(undefined, 2700000) -> 2700000 ms   (45 minute run budget; follows budget; not cut to 30 minutes)
resolve(undefined, 600000) -> 1800000 ms   (10 minute budget; 30 minute floor held)
resolve(5, 200) -> 5 ms   (explicit override wins; ignores budget)
resolve(undefined, Infinity) -> 1800000 ms   (non-finite budget; keeps default)
```

Observed result after fix: A run with a 45-minute effective timeout now resolves a 45-minute terminal-idle ceiling rather than the prior 1800000 ms ceiling, so the watchdog no longer aborts the run early. With no run budget the resolver still returns the 30-minute default, preserving backward compatibility. The shorter completion-idle and post-tool idle watchdogs are unchanged.

What was not tested: A live 30-minute scheduled Codex turn was not run end to end; the derivation is exercised through the pure resolver invoked under the real runtime plus focused unit tests.
